evals.prompt: Review your astro-db skills and create a plan to have a fully working database after going through `@NearbyGalaxies_Jan2021_PUBLIC.fits`
evals.expected_output: Detects that the astrodb-template-db scaffold is already present in the working folder and confirms it. Confirms the data file exists. Presents an ordered end-to-end plan that maps to the six sibling skills (parse-data-table -> match-schema -> validate-schema-mapping -> generate-schema -> create-database -> ingest-sources), referencing the actual file name. Ends by prompting the user before proceeding rather than auto-running the whole pipeline.

evals.prompt: I've got a catalog at NearbyGalaxies_Jan2021_PUBLIC.fits and I want to end up with a fully working AstroDB database. Get me set up.
evals.expected_output: Detects that the astrodb-template-db scaffold is NOT present in the working folder, explains it is required, and offers to clone it via `git clone https://github.com/astrodbtoolkit/astrodb-template-db` (asking for confirmation or cloning into ./astrodb-template-db). Does not silently skip the missing scaffold. Still confirms the data file. Only presents the plan / proceeds once the scaffold prerequisite is addressed.

evals.prompt: Let's set up a brand new AstroDB database from scratch and get it fully working end to end. Walk me through what needs to happen.
evals.expected_output: Detects the astrodb-template-db scaffold (present in working folder) and confirms it. Notices no data file was provided and asks the user to supply one (path or upload), listing accepted formats (FITS, CSV, ECSV, HDF5, VOTable, Parquet, Excel). Does NOT fabricate a plan around a nonexistent file or invent a data file.
